name: Technical Bomber Jacket
description: A clean, minimal bomber built for the in-between seasons — light enough for spring, weatherproof enough for a sudden downpour.
whyThisFitsMyArchive: The proportions are right straight off the rack — not oversized, not fitted. Navy reads as sophisticated rather than sporty. This is the jacket I actually reach for on an ordinary weekday.
whatItReplaces: Replaces a heavier, more structured jacket I used to over-pack for spring trips. This does the same job at half the bulk and actually handles a sudden downpour.
whyIChooseThis: Over heritage brand versions: same silhouette, realistic price, easy to replace. Over trend-driven jackets: this proportion won't look dated in five years.
whatItPairsWithInMyCollection: Over a Supima tee with raw denim and Samba. Works layered over the overshirt in colder weather.
layeringCombinations: Over the Supima tee. Over the linen shirt or overshirt when it's cooler than expected.
colourCombinations: Navy is the one I actually own and wear. Black and stone are realistic alternates if I need a second colourway.
costPerWearReasoning: ₹12,900, worn through both shoulder seasons most years. It replaces needing a separate rain jacket and a separate light jacket, so the real cost is lower than it looks on paper.
whatMakesItTimeless: A simple, correctly-proportioned jacket built for real bodies, not seasonal trend cycles.
whatProblemItSolves: A lightweight jacket that works spring and autumn without chasing fashion.
whatCouldReplaceIt: The wool coat for colder days, but this fills the in-between-season gap nothing else does.
layeringOver: the Supima tee, the overshirt, or the linen shirt
alternativeUnder3000: Zara Basic Bomber (~₹2,990 at zara.com/in) — thinner construction, similar silhouette
alternativeUnder10000: H&M Studio Satin Bomber (~₹5,990 at hm.com/in)
premiumAlternative: Norse Projects Ryan Nylon Bomber (~₹30,000 import)
buyRecommendation: Wait for Sale — outerwear has the deepest seasonal discounts. This can drop to ₹6,000-₹8,000 at clearance.
